other_observers: none; I found the bird. I am not sure if anyone else saw it. Apparently, Stan Oswald went to look for it on the same day. I didn't hear if he saw it.
description: I first saw this bird flying. It was obviously a pelican based on its very large spatulate bill, ponderous flight, and top-heavy appearance.   The overall coloration of the pelican was brown, not white. The dorsal suface was brown, the bill was dark, not yellowish, the ventral surface of the wings were dark on leading and trailing edges with whitisn in the center.   The belly was gray, the head dark without any yellow as in an adult.Behaviors: Fly and swim, but it did not plunge-dive or plow through the water with its bill open. When in flight, it was harrassed a couple of times by larger, not Franklin's Gulls.Call: nonePlumage: Immature
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
